headline: Notre-Dame stands restored, welcoming millions once more
meta_description: Notre-Dame de Paris reopened in December 2024 after a five-year restoration by thousands of artisans — visitors are pouring back.
summary_short: Five years after the fire, Paris's cathedral opened its doors again — rebuilt by thousands of craftspeople and visited by millions.
body:
When Notre-Dame burned in April 2019, the world watched the spire fall and feared the 860-year-old cathedral was lost. Five years later, the doors opened again. Around two thousand carpenters, stonemasons, roofers, gilders and restorers — many using medieval techniques — rebuilt the roof, raised a new spire and cleaned centuries of soot from the vaults.

Since the reopening in December 2024, millions of visitors have walked back into a luminous interior brighter than any living person had seen it. A building that took generations to raise was nearly lost in a night — and won back in five years of collective, painstaking devotion.
